message:
feat(benchmarks): add error classification and retry logic for benchmark execution

Introduces structured error handling and resilient retry policies for
benchmark collection, improving reliability of SpeedScore measurements
on flaky or overloaded workers.

BenchmarkError (error.rs):
- SSH connection/timeout errors (retryable)
- Resource exhaustion (reschedule for later)
- Disk full on worker (non-retryable)
- Compilation failures (non-retryable)
- Network benchmark failures (retryable)
- Invalid result data (non-retryable)
- Worker unreachable (retryable + reschedule)
- User cancellation (non-retryable)

Error classification:
- is_retryable(): Should attempt immediate retry with backoff
- should_reschedule(): Should queue for later attempt when worker
  resources free up (resource exhaustion, unreachable)

BenchmarkRetryPolicy (retry.rs):
- Configurable max attempts, base delay, max delay, jitter
- Exponential backoff with jitter to prevent thundering herd
- Default: 3 attempts, 5s base, 60s max, 20% jitter

run_with_retry() helper:
- Generic async retry executor for benchmark phases
- Structured logging of attempt progress
- Backoff sleep between retries
- Early exit on non-retryable errors

Benchmark module updates:
- Re-export error and retry types
- Minor CPU and network benchmark adjustments for error integration

This infrastructure supports the benchmark scheduling system (bead wpk)
and improves overall telemetry collection reliability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
